summaries: Between 14:30 and 17:45 PDT on 10 Apr 2026, customers using {service} on Amazon EKS in the us-west-2 Region experienced elevated error rates coinciding with a traffic surge. Request throughput reached {rps} RPS, approximately {traffic_mult}x the provisioned baseline of {baseline_rps} RPS. Container memory rose in lockstep with inbound load, peaking at {mem_used} of {mem_limit} before pod {pod} was evicted. Error rates reached {error_pct}%. The issue resolved as traffic subsided.

Tracking ID: AZ-TR4-20260411 — Between approximately 09:15 and 12:00 UTC on 11 Apr 2026, a subset of customers using Azure Kubernetes Service in the West US 2 region experienced degraded performance for {service}. An unexpected traffic surge drove per-pod memory from baseline to {mem_used}, triggering OOM evictions. Scaling from {from_replicas} to {to_replicas} replicas at 11:30 UTC reduced per-pod load and resolved the issue.

02:10 AM PDT — We are investigating elevated error rates for {service} in the us-east1 region. Inbound traffic increased to {rps} RPS ({traffic_mult}x the {baseline_rps} RPS baseline) beginning at approximately 01:45 AM PDT. Pod {pod} has been evicted due to memory exhaustion. We are scaling the deployment to absorb the additional load.

Google Cloud Status — The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine affecting {service} in us-central1 has been resolved as of 16:55 US/Pacific. Between 13:20 and 16:55 US/Pacific, a traffic surge to {rps} RPS caused memory utilization on individual pods to reach {mem_used} ({mem_pct}% of the {mem_limit} limit). OOM evictions ceased after the deployment was scaled from {from_replicas} to {to_replicas} replicas.

Tracking ID: 8_N7-PQ2 — Summary of impact: Between approximately 20:00 and 23:30 UTC on 12 Apr 2026, customers accessing {service} on Azure Container Apps in North Europe experienced elevated latency and error rates.
